storyDialogue.text: Get started! Use ‣ AUTO for automatic scene change, LEFT/RIGHT icon for manual navigation, and toggle music/voice on the top right icon.
via the options menu, You can change Typing speed and other settings.

[shake] The Kitagawa Academy clock tower strikes 6:00 PM. You are {{user}}, the only person in this building who realizes that 'Student Council' is just Japanese for 'Unpaid Intern.' You're currently drowning in Shion’s obsession with font sizes.
The budget reports aren't just numbers, {{user}}. They're a statement. And your statement currently says 'I don't give a damn about alignment.'
Jesus, Shion. Relax. It’s a high school budget, not a fucking IMF proposal. Nobody’s checking the kerning on the volleyball club’s equipment request.
[glitch] Precision is power. If I let one comma slide, the whole system rots. You think Takahashi transferred because of his 'family'? He transferred because he couldn't handle the discipline.
He transferred because you sent him a fourteen-page critique of his handwriting. The kid had a panic attack in the infirmary, Shion. You’re not a leader; you’re a dictator in a pleated skirt.
[blue_tint] The hallway vending machine groans, spitting out a can of liquid grass. The air is thick with the scent of ozone and Shion’s failing grip on reality.
Drink this. Unsweetened green tea. It tastes like boiled lawn clipping—right up your alley. Maybe it’ll stop your brain from hemorrhaging over the club allocation sheets.
[blink] It's... traditional, Ren. It builds character. Unlike that carbonated poison you drink.
Shion, look at your bag. You literally tried to eat a graphing calculator for lunch because you forgot what food looks like. This isn't 'responsibility.' It's a slow-motion suicide.
I can't stop. If I'm not perfect, what's left? Just another seventeen-year-old girl with no plan. I'm not like you, Ren. I don't have the luxury of failing.
[sepia] The sky outside is a violent, bleeding orange. The shadows of the cherry trees stretch across the floor like long, grasping fingers. The silence in the room is heavy—dangerous.
The Cultural Festival timeline accounts for a 15% margin of error in vendor deliveries and—
Shut up. Just... shut up for one second. Look at the sky. It’s dying, Shion. And it’s the most beautiful thing you’ll see all week.
It’s just Rayleigh scattering, Ren. Light hitting the atmosphere at an angle. It doesn't mean anything.
It means the world is moving on without us while you’re worrying about a fucking spreadsheet. Put the pen down. Give me five minutes, or I’m walking out and taking the budget files with me.

[electricuted] A power struggle in the quiet of the third floor. Shion’s hand trembles over her keyboard. The 'Delete' key is a heartbeat away from total chaos. She looks at the sky, then at Ren's hand on the window sill.
...Fine. Five minutes. But if we miss the printer deadline, I'm killing you first.
Deal. Now sit. Watch the light fade. It's the only thing in this room you can't control.

[nuke] The sun dips below the horizon, dragging the day into the dark. In the fading light, the student council room feels less like an office and more like a confessional. The clock ticks. The reports remain unfinished. For once, Shion doesn't fix the comma.
